description: Recreate the TradingCat harness scaffold for the market-awareness request and record the current starting blockers before feature work.
steps: Keep a fresh `PLAN.json` with immutable descriptions/steps for the market-awareness scope.
Keep `PROGRESS.md` append-only with the restored session facts and baseline failures.
Commit the harness initialization checkpoint before implementation work starts.

description: Reproduce and pin the current API baseline failures that must be cleared before market-awareness delivery can be trusted.
steps: Run `./.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_api.py -q` from the repo root.
Record the exact failing test names and primary causes in `PROGRESS.md`.
Use the failing list as the pre-feature stabilization gate.

description: Stabilize research report, scorecard, and strategy-detail generation when live Futu option-chain requests are throttled or quote rights are missing.
steps: Keep `/research/report/run`, `/research/scorecard/run`, and `/research/strategies/{id}` returning `200` under default safe mode.
Prefer local-history or deterministic fallback paths before making live option-chain requests in safe/test flows.
Re-run the research interface tests and confirm the rate-limit failure no longer occurs.

description: Restore RSI trigger evaluation so `/ops/evaluate-triggers` uses synced market history instead of stale fallback values.
steps: Reproduce `test_ops_evaluate_triggers_uses_real_rsi_series`.
Fix the indicator lookup path so synced SPY history drives the RSI metric value.
Verify the trigger evaluation payload includes the live `RSI_14` metric value and triggers as expected.

description: Restore SMA trigger evaluation so `/ops/evaluate-triggers` uses synced market history instead of stale fallback values.
steps: Reproduce `test_ops_evaluate_triggers_uses_real_sma_series`.
Fix the SMA metric lookup path so synced SPY history drives the `SMA_10` value.
Verify the trigger evaluation payload includes the live SMA value and triggers as expected.

description: Restore order listing price-context fields after manual reconciliation.
steps: Reproduce `test_orders_endpoint_exposes_expected_vs_realized_price_context`.
Make `/orders` expose `expected_price`, `realized_price`, and `reference_source` for reconciled manual orders.
Verify the manual-order plus manual-fill flow returns the expected values end to end.

description: Restore persistent-universe strategy detail behavior and indicator snapshot exposure.
steps: Reproduce `test_research_strategy_details_follow_persistent_universe_and_expose_indicator_snapshots`.
Ensure research strategy details follow the persisted instrument universe instead of drifting back to hard-coded samples.
Verify indicator snapshots remain present on the strategy detail payload.

description: Restore the default `/execution/run` request contract so an empty JSON body returns a valid execution summary.
steps: Reproduce `test_execution_run_endpoint`.
Make the endpoint accept `{}` without a `422` contract failure.
Verify the response still exposes `signal_count`, `intent_count`, `approval_count`, submitted/failed orders, and gate context.

description: Define the product contract for the new market-awareness capability in operator-facing terms before implementation.
steps: Lock the core outcome as: infer market posture from trend structure and translate it into operator guidance.
State that the feature must stay advisory-only and must not silently place or approve trades.
Document the contract in `.harness/spec.md` and mirror the execution sequence in `PROGRESS.md`.

description: Lock the benchmark basket and reference universe used to judge market posture across US, HK, CN, and cross-asset context.
steps: Choose default benchmark symbols for each market plus defensive cross-asset references.
Prefer persisted/tradable instruments when possible and use fallback samples only when the universe is empty.
Document the basket so later harness rounds do not change it implicitly.

description: Lock the regime taxonomy and confidence labels that the service will emit.
steps: Use a finite posture set such as `bullish`, `neutral`, `caution`, and `risk_off`.
Define confidence semantics so mixed evidence is explicit instead of hidden.
Keep the labels stable for API, dashboard, and test assertions.

description: Lock the operator recommendation tiers and priority rules that map from regime outputs to concrete advice.
steps: Define recommendation tiers such as build risk, hold pace, reduce risk, and pause new adds.
Specify how conflicting signals collapse into one prioritized action list.
Keep the mapping deterministic so the same market inputs yield the same advice.

description: Add typed API/view models for the top-level market-awareness snapshot.
steps: Define a response model that includes `as_of`, overall regime, confidence, posture, and score.
Keep the response compatible with the existing FastAPI/Pydantic response-model pattern.
Verify the model can serialize both full and degraded payloads cleanly.

description: Add typed models for per-market lenses and evidence rows.
steps: Represent each market view with benchmark symbol, regime, score, and supporting metrics.
Represent evidence rows with signal name, status, numeric value, and explanation.
Keep the shape stable enough for dashboard tables and badge rendering.

description: Add typed models for strategy guidance and operator action items.
steps: Represent each action with severity, action key, human-readable text, and rationale.
Represent each strategy guidance row with strategy id, stance, and regime-specific advice.
Keep the model explicit so downstream UI code does not infer semantics from free-form text.

description: Add typed models for market-awareness data quality and degraded-state reporting.
steps: Expose whether the snapshot is complete, degraded, or fallback-driven.
List missing symbols, stale windows, or live-adapter limitations in structured form.
Keep degraded payloads valid under the response model instead of crashing or returning partial ad hoc dicts.

description: Add application-config defaults for market-awareness benchmark symbols and lookback windows.
steps: Add defaults for short, medium, and long trend windows.
Add defaults for the benchmark symbols per market and cross-asset references.
Keep the new config optional so existing environments start without `.env` changes.

description: Add config defaults for thresholds that control breadth, drawdown, volatility, and overlay weighting.
steps: Define sensible defaults for breadth pass ratios and volatility stress lines.
Define drawdown and momentum threshold defaults used by the posture engine.
Keep the defaults centralized instead of scattering literals across the service.

description: Implement a local-history-first loader for benchmark price series used by the posture engine.
steps: Pull benchmark histories from the existing market-history service before attempting live fetches.
Normalize missing history handling into structured blocker output.
Verify the loader works for at least one symbol from US, HK, and CN contexts.

description: Implement a universe loader that prefers persisted tradable instruments for breadth calculations.
steps: Read the persisted instrument catalog from the existing repository/service layer.
Filter by enabled, tradable, and liquid defaults for posture use.
Fallback to sample instruments only when the persisted universe is empty and mark that in the payload.

description: Implement market-specific breadth constituent selection helpers.
steps: Split the breadth universe by market and supported asset classes.
Exclude option contracts and other unsuitable instruments from breadth counts.
Verify the helper returns deterministic symbol lists for the same stored universe.

description: Compute trend-alignment features from benchmark price versus moving averages.
steps: Calculate benchmark posture against short, medium, and long moving averages.
Expose the alignment result as structured evidence rather than only one score.
Verify the feature behaves correctly on rising and falling synthetic or cached series.

description: Compute multi-horizon momentum features for the benchmark basket.
steps: Calculate 21-day, 63-day, and 126-day momentum or equivalent windows.
Normalize the outputs so cross-market comparisons remain interpretable.
Verify positive and negative momentum cases map to the expected evidence rows.

description: Compute drawdown-stress features from rolling highs.
steps: Measure current drawdown versus recent rolling peaks for each benchmark.
Escalate the stress classification when drawdown crosses configured thresholds.
Verify drawdown evidence feeds the market posture instead of remaining informational only.

description: Compute realized-volatility and stability features for the benchmark basket.
steps: Measure recent volatility over a fixed rolling window.
Map elevated volatility into caution or risk-off pressure.
Verify calm and stressed volatility regimes produce different evidence and advice.

description: Compute breadth features from the persisted tradable universe.
steps: Measure the share of symbols above key moving averages or equivalent trend gates.
Keep the breadth calculation bounded when the market-specific universe is small.
Verify breadth degrades gracefully when symbol coverage is sparse.

description: Compute cross-asset confirmation using defensive assets versus equity risk assets.
steps: Compare equities with bonds, gold, or other defensive references already used by the repo.
Use the result to confirm or challenge the equity-only trend reading.
Expose the cross-asset confirmation explicitly in the evidence list.

description: Compute market-calendar and session-phase context for operator timing advice.
steps: Reuse the existing market-calendar service for open, pre-open, and closed states.
Attach session context to market-specific caution notes.
Verify the service can explain when posture is supportive but the session is not actionable yet.

description: Compute a macro-calendar pressure overlay as context, not as the primary posture signal.
steps: Reuse the existing macro-calendar service to flag near-term event pressure.
Convert upcoming event clusters into caution annotations rather than replacing trend logic.
Verify the overlay disappears cleanly when no events are present.

description: Compute an optional alpha-radar sentiment overlay with graceful fallback behavior.
steps: Reuse the existing alpha-radar service or data shape without making it a hard dependency.
Treat the overlay as secondary evidence that can raise or lower confidence modestly.
Verify missing or synthetic radar data does not break the posture calculation.

description: Normalize all evidence into comparable subscores and machine-readable evidence rows.
steps: Convert trend, momentum, drawdown, volatility, breadth, and overlays into bounded subscores.
Emit evidence rows with numeric value, status, and explanation for every subscore.
Keep the normalization deterministic and transparent for tests and UI debugging.

description: Aggregate the evidence subscores into per-market regime scores and confidence values.
steps: Produce one posture result each for US, HK, and CN when data exists.
Keep per-market confidence lower when inputs are sparse or degraded.
Verify conflicting evidence produces mixed or lower-confidence outcomes instead of silently forcing bullish or bearish.

description: Aggregate per-market regimes into one overall market posture for the operator.
steps: Combine market-level outputs with cross-asset confirmation into one top-level regime.
Keep the aggregation rule explicit and testable.
Verify the top-level posture changes when multiple market views deteriorate together.

description: Expose degraded status when history coverage, quote rights, or live adapters are incomplete.
steps: Mark the snapshot degraded when required history or quotes are unavailable.
Include structured blocker details and missing symbols in the response.
Verify degraded snapshots still render and still produce conservative advice.

description: Translate the overall regime into a portfolio-level risk posture.
steps: Map each regime to a posture such as add risk, maintain, de-risk, or defensive only.
Keep the posture advisory and separate from execution permission or order placement.
Verify the posture is present in the API and dashboard summary.

description: Generate concrete operator actions for entries, adds, trims, hedging, and waiting.
steps: Emit a prioritized action list with explicit rationale and severity.
Keep action text concise enough for dashboard use but specific enough to act on.
Verify bullish, neutral, and risk-off regimes each produce materially different action lists.

description: Generate strategy-specific guidance for the ETF rotation strategy.
steps: Translate posture into advice on broad ETF adds, defensive switches, or patience for Strategy A.
Reference the strategy by id so the UI can render guidance beside the existing strategy metadata.
Verify risk-off posture does not recommend aggressive ETF rotation adds.

description: Generate strategy-specific guidance for the equity momentum strategy.
steps: Translate posture into advice on new stock adds, tightening filters, or standing down for Strategy B.
Make the guidance sensitive to breadth deterioration and volatility spikes.
Verify neutral or caution posture tempers momentum adds instead of always saying keep buying.

description: Generate strategy-specific guidance for the option overlay strategy.
steps: Translate posture into advice on protective puts, covered calls, or reduced option activity for Strategy C.
Respect the repo's existing option-budget and hedging boundaries.
Verify risk-off posture can recommend more defense while bullish posture does not over-expand option risk.

description: Generate market-specific caution notes for CN, HK, and US execution timing.
steps: Use market session status and regime confidence to produce actionable caution notes.
Include timing notes such as pre-open wait, open-session confirm, or closed-session review.
Verify each market note is absent or explicit rather than implied by UI-only logic.

description: Inject market-awareness summary into daily trading plan generation so the operator sees posture guidance in the main workflow.
steps: Surface the overall posture and top actions when building the daily plan note or fallback plan summary.
Keep the plan note advisory-only and do not auto-edit target weights or approvals.
Verify the trading-plan payload includes the market-awareness summary when a plan is generated.

description: Add a dedicated research-query entrypoint for market-awareness snapshots.
steps: Wire the new service through the query-service layer instead of embedding logic in routes or facades.
Keep the query path callable from both API routes and dashboard assembly.
Verify the query layer accepts an `as_of` date and returns the typed payload.

description: Expose the new feature via `GET /research/market-awareness` and `POST /research/market-awareness/run`.
steps: Add thin route handlers that delegate into the query/facade layer.
Return a stable response model under both GET and POST patterns to match the repo's research conventions.
Verify both routes return `200` and the expected top-level fields.

description: Keep the market-awareness API stable when live adapters degrade or rate-limit.
steps: Catch adapter failures and convert them into degraded payload fields instead of uncaught exceptions.
Prefer local history and cached context before live calls in safe mode.
Verify the endpoint still returns structured advice when optional live overlays fail.

description: Add market-awareness data into the dashboard summary payload under `details`.
steps: Extend the dashboard summary context with one embedded market-awareness snapshot.
Keep the payload shape explicit so frontend code does not make extra hidden fetches for core cards.
Verify `/dashboard/summary` returns the new `details.market_awareness` object.

description: Surface market-awareness loading in the research dashboard page flow.
steps: Update the research page data-loading path to fetch the new endpoint or use the embedded summary.
Avoid redundant network calls when the needed data is already in the dashboard summary payload.
Verify the research page can load the posture cards without breaking the existing candidate tables.

description: Add market-awareness overview cards to the research dashboard page.
steps: Render top-level regime, confidence, and posture badges in a clearly visible panel.
Keep the visual language consistent with the existing dashboard card system.
Verify the cards render meaningful content on both desktop and narrow layouts.

description: Add an evidence table for per-market and per-signal posture drivers.
steps: Render the per-market lenses and evidence rows in a scan-friendly table or list.
Expose signal status and explanation without requiring users to inspect raw JSON.
Verify evidence rows render for at least one bullish and one degraded snapshot.

description: Add operator action and strategy-guidance panels to the research dashboard page.
steps: Render prioritized action items with severity styling.
Render one guidance row per live strategy family so the feature maps to existing workflows.
Verify the UI does not imply automatic trade execution or approvals.

description: Add degraded-state and no-data UI handling for market-awareness panels.
steps: Render a clear fallback state when the snapshot is degraded or empty.
Show blocker text and missing-data hints without breaking the surrounding research page.
Verify the page stays usable when only partial market-awareness data is available.

description: Add service-level coverage for bullish or risk-on posture classification.
steps: Create a deterministic fixture that yields strong upward trend and supportive breadth.
Assert the overall posture becomes bullish or risk-on with supportive actions.
Assert the evidence rows explain why the posture is supportive.

description: Add service-level coverage for neutral or mixed posture classification.
steps: Create a fixture with mixed trend and breadth evidence.
Assert the service returns a neutral or caution result with reduced confidence.
Assert the action list recommends pacing or waiting instead of aggressive adds.

description: Add service-level coverage for bearish or risk-off posture classification.
steps: Create a fixture with broken trend, weak breadth, and elevated stress.
Assert the service returns risk-off posture and de-risking guidance.
Assert strategy guidance turns defensive for ETF, momentum, and options sleeves.

description: Add service-level coverage for degraded and missing-data posture classification.
steps: Create a fixture with incomplete history or missing benchmarks.
Assert the snapshot returns degraded status with explicit blockers.
Assert the advice becomes conservative instead of failing open.

description: Add service-level coverage for strategy-guidance mapping.
steps: Assert ETF, momentum, and option guidance each respond differently to the same posture.
Keep strategy ids and stance labels stable in the test assertions.
Verify the guidance text stays advisory-only.

description: Add API contract coverage for `/research/market-awareness`.
steps: Assert GET and POST routes both return `200`.
Assert the payload includes overall posture, market views, evidence, actions, strategy guidance, and data-quality fields.
Assert degraded snapshots still satisfy the response contract.

description: Add dashboard summary contract coverage for embedded market-awareness data.
steps: Assert `/dashboard/summary` exposes `details.market_awareness`.
Assert the embedded payload includes posture and action fields needed by the UI.
Keep existing dashboard contract assertions passing.

description: Extend page and static-asset coverage for the new market-awareness UI sections.
steps: Assert the research page HTML includes the new market-awareness panel anchors.
Assert the relevant static JS file references the market-awareness API/data path.
Keep existing dashboard/research page smoke assertions green.

description: Run focused pytest coverage for the restored baseline and new market-awareness paths.
steps: Run the focused API and service tests that cover the baseline regressions and the new feature.
Record command lines and results in `PROGRESS.md`.
Do not mark the feature complete until the focused suite is green.

description: Run a local application smoke and verify the market-awareness output end to end.
steps: Start the app with the repo's safe local workflow.
Use `curl` to verify `/research/market-awareness` and `/dashboard/summary` return the new fields.
Verify the research dashboard page visibly renders the posture cards and guidance panels, using browser-style validation when available and HTML/API smoke otherwise.
